skill.name: datadata-dql
skill.description: Helps write Datadata DQL scripts for data transformation, cleaning, SQL querying, HTTP requests, and tabular analysis.
skill.summary: Create Datadata DQL scripts with Starlark-style syntax, DataFrame operations, SQL queries, and HTTP integrations.
skill.author: datadata-team

security_audit.summary: Static external-command and weak-crypto alerts were evaluated as false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code, and type-stub documentation. The skill intentionally documents outbound HTTP through fetch(), including authenticated requests and examples that send query results to external APIs, so publication should include a data egress warning.

medium_findings.title: Outbound HTTP and Data Egress Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill teaches DQL users to call external URLs with fetch(). One example combines query() results containing user identifiers and email addresses with a POST to an external API. This is legitimate for integrations, but generated scripts could expose internal data if users choose untrusted endpoints or include sensitive fields.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly presents outbound HTTP as a core capability and includes a database-to-external-API sync example. This is not malicious by itself, but the data egress path is explicit.

medium_findings.title: Credential-Like Placeholder Examples
medium_findings.description: The HTTP reference includes examples with an Authorization bearer token placeholder and a form body containing username and password values. These are example credentials, not real secrets, but users should avoid embedding real credentials in generated scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values appear to be placeholders in documentation, so confirmed harm is low. The pattern is still relevant because the skill helps generate scripts that may include headers and request bodies.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backticks as Ruby or shell execution. Review showed these are Markdown inline-code markers and fenced DQL examples, not executable shell commands in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is documentation syntax and sample DQL, with no script file or runtime path that executes shell commands. No evidence found for subprocess, shell, or OS command execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts matched ordinary API documentation terms such as hash-like wording, statistical mode, and smoothing alpha. No evidence found for cryptographic code, digest generation, or use of weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected locations are type-stub and data-analysis documentation, not cryptographic implementations. The scanner labels do not match semantic context.

low_findings.title: SQL Query Guidance Includes Safer Patterns
low_findings.description: The skill can generate SQL queries through query(), which can be risky if users concatenate untrusted input. The reference explicitly documents parameterized query placeholders, reducing the concern to normal SQL-script review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The query capability is real, but the documentation directly recommends parameterized SQL. No evidence found that the skill instructs users to bypass safe query construction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Scripts May Send Queried Data to External APIs
dangerous_patterns.description: The fetch() reference shows query() output being serialized and posted to an external sync endpoint. This pattern should require user confirmation of destination, fields, and data sensitivity before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented and combines local query data with outbound HTTP. It is a legitimate integration pattern with clear data exposure risk.

content.value_statement: Datadata users often need correct DQL syntax for cleaning, querying, and transforming data. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce DQL scripts using the included API references.
actual_capabilities: Explains required DQL return statements and Starlark-style syntax limits.
Guides SQL query() usage, including parameterized queries and result handling.
Supports DataFrame and Series transformations, filtering, aggregation, merging, and sorting.
Documents fetch() for HTTP GET, POST, headers, request bodies, and response parsing.
References built-in JSON, math, time, error handling, and canvas drawing APIs.
Encourages stepwise validation by returning intermediate data samples.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, but it does not execute or validate DQL scripts.
External API schemas and business rules still require user confirmation.
Generated scripts may need adjustment for Datadata platform permissions and available tables.
HTTP examples can expose data if users send sensitive fields to untrusted endpoints.

use_cases.title: Clean Operational Data
use_cases.description: Create DQL scripts that load table data, remove duplicates, validate required fields, and return clean DataFrame results.

use_cases.title: Build Metric Queries
use_cases.description: Generate SQL-backed DQL scripts for revenue, activity, retention, and other recurring business metrics.

use_cases.title: Integrate API Data
use_cases.description: Draft DQL workflows that call external APIs, inspect response shapes, and convert JSON or CSV responses into tables.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Query Script
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user needs a simple DQL query with a safe return value.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Datadata DQL script that queries [table name], selects [columns], limits results to [row count], and returns the DataFrame.

prompt_templates.title: Clean and Validate a Table
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs repeatable data cleaning with clear error handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DQL script for [table name] that checks required columns [columns], removes duplicate [key], filters invalid rows, and returns a cleaned DataFrame.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Time-Based Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs grouped metrics over dates or timestamps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a DQL script that calculates [metric] by [time period] from [table], handles missing values, and returns the latest [number] periods.

prompt_templates.title: Fetch and Merge External Data
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs API data combined with Datadata query results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a DQL script that fetches [API URL], first returns a sample for schema confirmation, then merges confirmed fields with query results from [table].

output_examples.input: Request: Build a DQL script that lists active users and validates required columns.
output_examples.output: A script outline that queries active users, checks id and email fields, removes duplicate ids, and returns the cleaned table.

output_examples.input: Request: Analyze monthly revenue growth for this year.
output_examples.output: A DQL workflow that queries monthly revenue, compares current and previous periods, calculates growth percent, and returns a compact summary.

output_examples.input: Request: Load product data from an API and convert it to a table.
output_examples.output: A two-step approach that first returns the response type and sample data, then converts confirmed product records into a DataFrame.

best_practices: Read the relevant reference file before asking for a complete DQL script.
Return intermediate samples first when table fields or API response shapes are uncertain.
Use parameterized query placeholders and confirm outbound HTTP destinations before sending data.
anti_patterns: Do not omit the final return statement, because DQL scripts otherwise return empty results.
Do not guess external API field order or nested response structure.
Do not place real credentials or sensitive user data in HTTP examples without explicit approval.

faq.question: What is Datadata DQL?
faq.answer: DQL is a Datadata scripting language based on Starlark with added data, SQL, HTTP, JSON, math, and time APIs.

faq.question: Does this skill run DQL scripts?
faq.answer: No. It helps write scripts and reference APIs. Execution happens inside the Datadata platform.

faq.question: Can it write SQL queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents query() and recommends parameterized placeholders for user-controlled values.

faq.question: Can it call external APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents fetch() for HTTP requests. Review destinations and data fields before sending information externally.

faq.question: Why does the skill ask to inspect data first?
faq.answer: The references recommend returning sample data first when response structure, field order, or business rules are uncertain.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
